note: These 5 remaining overlay components are a DIFFERENT SHAPE from the 7 declarative overlays already done (dropdown-menu/select/dialog/popover/combobox/menu-bar/autocomplete). They are imperative / hook-driven / calendar-grid, which the static-canonical-fixture + Ark-data-part pattern does not cleanly fit. Recorded honestly rather than fabricating non-representative fixtures or thrashing. None are permanently impossible; each needs a different capture approach.

toasty.reason: Kumo Toast is IMPERATIVE-only: <Toast.Provider> + Toast.useToastManager(); you call toast(...) to show one. There is no declarative <Toast.Root> to render a stable canonical fixture. Capturing requires orchestrating the manager to emit a toast at render time and waiting for its enter animation to settle — a different harness than the declarative components.
toasty.arkEquivalent: @ark-ui/<fw>/toast (Toaster + createToaster) — also imperative; mappable but needs an imperative capture harness.

sidebar.reason: Sidebar is a LAYOUT SYSTEM driven by useSidebar() hook + <Sidebar.Provider> with expanded/collapsed/mobile states from context, not a single overlay. Canonical fixture must set up the provider + drive state; it is bespoke (no direct Ark primitive — Ark has no 'sidebar').
sidebar.arkEquivalent: none (bespoke; mostly presentation + a collapse toggle — could be native, not Ark)

date-picker.reason: Built on react-day-picker; a large calendar grid with mode=single|multiple|range and useState. Canonical fixture is stateful; the rendered grid is big. Ark @ark-ui/<fw>/date-picker exists and is mappable, but the calendar DOM is large and the two libraries' day-grid structure differs (tier C handles structure, but this is a substantial per-state fixture).

date-range-picker.reason: Same as date-picker (range mode); large calendar grid, react-day-picker based, stateful.

command-palette.reason: A combobox-in-a-dialog: <CommandPalette.Dialog open> wrapping <CommandPalette.Panel items>. Nested overlay (dialog + combobox + zone picker). Mappable as Ark dialog + combobox composed, but it is the most complex composition.

recommendation: Build the live dog-food homepage NOW with the 7 done overlays + the presentational/form set (a real, honest, interactive demo of the compiler working across Vue/Svelte/Solid). Then return for these 5 with shape-appropriate capture harnesses (imperative-toast harness, hook-driven sidebar, calendar-grid date pickers, composed command-palette) — each is its own focused effort, not a blocker to a visible demo.
